title: campanula pamphylica subsp. tokurii
intro: Campanula pamphylica subsp. tokurii, also known as the Turkish bellflower, is a plant species within the Campanula genus. It is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Turkey, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Campanula pamphylica subsp. tokurii include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and exhibiting potential anticancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional Turkish cooking and as a garnish for various dishes.
